start_cue: Protein first, creatine only after protein is dialed. Creatine amplifies training output — it has nothing to multiply if protein is insufficient.

weeks.focus: Hit the daily total — bodyweight in grams
weeks.detail: Target roughly bodyweight in pounds, expressed as grams of protein per day. A 180-pound person aims for ~180g daily. Do not yet worry about distribution across meals or post-workout timing — just hit the daily number consistently. Whole-food sources are not superior to protein powder; use whichever actually gets you to the number.

weeks.focus: Distribute across 3-5 meals with a per-meal cap
weeks.detail: Spread intake across three to five meals, each roughly 30-50g (bodyweight ÷ 4 is the practical per-meal ceiling). Multiple daily stimulations of muscle protein synthesis outperform one or two very large protein meals. Lock in a post-workout meal or shake within 60 minutes of finishing training — not rigidly, but consistently.

Only now, with protein reliably in place, add 5g of creatine monohydrate per day. No loading phase required — saturation happens in 3-4 weeks on the standard dose. Time of day does not matter. Expect slight intracellular water retention (visible as fuller muscles, not softer face). Creatine monohydrate only — the flashier forms cost more and work no better.

graduation: When protein hits without active counting most days, meal distribution is automatic, and creatine is a boring daily default, the supplement layer is doing its job. Further supplementation (vitamin D, omega-3, magnesium) becomes reasonable — everything else in the category stays optional and evidence-limited.
